Rung.Comment:
============================================================ T_LES: TIME -- LESS THAN ============================================================ Revision 1.0-Example: 2008-Jul-02, DER Release for V17 CD as Date/Time Examples ============================================================ Returns TRUE (1) in EnableOut and Out if Ref_DT1 is LESS THAN (before) Ref_DT2 ============================================================ POWERUP / INVALID STATE HANDLING ============================================================ This logic determines a comparison result every scan when enabled (and sets EnableOut and Out to 0 every scan if on a False Rung or if EnableIn is cleared). No powerup or invalid state handling is necessary. ============================================================ NORMALIZE THE INPUT DATES/TIMES ============================================================ This rung adds each of the input Date/Time parameters to a . The T_ADD instruction its output to: 0 <= microseconds < 1.000,000 0 <= seconds < 60 0 <= minutes < 60 0 <= hours < 24 1 <= days <= 31 (and day is valid for calendar month/year) 1 <= months <= 12 valid years are about +/- 5.8 million
Remember that if A < B, then A+C < B+C, so we are OK to add. And it works if what we're adding is a Date/Time OR an Amount of Time!!!

============================================================ COMPARE Wrk_DT1 < Wrk_DT2 ============================================================ Now that the two input parameters have been : IF the Wrk_DT1 YEAR is lower, or if the years are the same and the Wrk_DT1 MONTH is lower, or if the years and months are the same and the Wrk_DT1 DAY is lower, or if the years, months and days are the same and the Wrk_DT1 HOUR is lower, or if the years, months, days and hours are the same and the Wrk_DT1 MINUTE is lower, or if the years, months, days, hours and minutes are the same and the Wrk_DT1 SECOND is lower, or if the years, months, days, hours, minutes and seconds are the same and the Wrk_DT1 MICROSECOND is lower, (whew!) THEN Wrk_DT1 is BEFORE (less than) Wrk_DT2, and so Ref_DT1 is less than Ref_DT2.
(NOTE: You can type <ALT>- 0 (zero) or click the icon on the toolbar to close the Controller Organizer tree if you would like a wider view of this rung.)

Normally there would be an OTE of on the rung above, but because we unlatch (OTU) the Out bit in the EnableInFalse routine, we need to have all references be OTL or OTU in order to avoid an annoying verification Warning (duplicate destructive bit reference). This warning appears in the user program even if this AOI is locked down....
